items.title: Bloomberg Invest | Day One | March 4 8:45 am
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>When it comes to your world, your money and your burning questions, you want answers from leaders who know how to build empires, protect portfolios and create the next big thing.

Held in the heart of New York’s Financial District, Bloomberg Invest is the essential event that convenes allocators, dealmakers and investors from across the globe. Powered by one of the world’s largest newsrooms and led by Global Finance Correspondent Sonali Basak, join us to track, dissect and navigate the markets with the industry’s most influential voices.

Here are some of the speakers you'll hear from:
Lynn Martin, President, NYSE Group 
Marc Lipschultz, Co-CEO, Blue Owl Capital
Dawn Fitzpatrick, CEO & Chief Investment Officer, Soros Fund Management 
Gary D. Cohn, Vice Chairman, IBM

#business #investing #stocks</article>

items.title: Leaning into Opportunity in Political, Economic Volatility: Ryan Tolkin
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Ryan Tolkin, CEO at Schonfeld Strategic Advisers, discusses how he's looking to capitalize on a volatile landscape as policy changes and tariffs out of Washington elicit reaction from the market.
--------

items.title: El-Erian Sees Increased Recession Risk, One Fed Rate Cut
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Mohamed El-Erian, Queens’ College Cambridge president and Bloomberg Opinion columnist, says he expects downward economic revisions in the coming weeks and explains why he thinks the probability of a recession has increased. El-Erian's opinions are his own.

Sign up for the Economics Daily newsletter to discover what's driving the global economy and what it means for policy makers, businesses, investors and you: https://bloom.bg/4535pfS

items.title: Tariff War Sparks Quick Retaliation on US | Balance of Power
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Hours before addressing Congress President Trump implements tariffs against Canada and Mexico, and retaliation has already begun. OPIS Global Head of Energy Analysis Tom Kloza, Stonecourt Capital LP Rick Davis, Center for the Study of the Presidency and Congress Jeanne Sheehan Zaino, Representative Mark Alford, Former USTR General Counsel Greta Peisch join "Balance of Power" to discuss the news coming out of Washington.

items.title: Canada Strikes Back Against US With Its Own Tariffs
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content: <article>Canada's Justin Trudeau says the US has started a trade war. Trudeau’s government hit back against President Donald Trump's tariffs with levies targeting C$30 billion ($20.8 billion) in products including cosmetics, appliances, tires, fruit and wine. BNN Bloomberg Contributor Jon Erlichman reports on what's next for Trudeau.</article>

items.title: Superpower Trade War Heats Up, Tariff Rift, AI Investing | Bloomberg Open Interest
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>A special edition of Bloomberg Open Interest is live from the heart of the financial district at Bloomberg Invest. Hosts Matt Miller, Sonali Basak and Katie Greifeld will be joined by executives, dealmakers and investors from across the globe who will weigh in on tariffs, inflation and the evolving role of AI in markets. Guests include Lynn Martin, NYSE Group President, Jonathan Lewinsohn, Diameter Capital Partners Co-Founder, Marc Lipshultz, Co-CEO of Blue Own Capital and Bin Ren, SigTech Founder and CEO.

items.title: Fed's Williams Says Tariffs Will Likely Spur Inflation
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Federal Reserve Bank of New York President John Williams says he anticipates President Donald Trump's tariffs will contribute to inflation, but emphasized there is a lot of uncertainty about how the economy will respond to them. He speaks at the Bloomberg Invest conference in New York.

items.title: Welcome to the Trade War | Bloomberg Surveillance | March 4, 2025
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Bloomberg Surveillance hosted by Tom Keene and Paul Sweeney
March 4, 2025 (with guest co-host Jess Menton)

Featuring:
•  Brendan Murray, Bloomberg News Trade Czar, reports on the economic fallout of the US-led trade war. President Donald Trump imposed 25% tariffs on most Canadian and Mexican imports and raised the charge on China to 20%, affecting roughly $1.5 trillion in annual imports

•   Barbara Reinhard, CIO of Multi Asset Strategies & Solutions at Voya Investment Management, discusses why she believes it's time to buy equities and potential investment options for deploying cash that is currently on the sidelines

•   Abby Joseph Cohen, professor at Columbia Business School and former senior investment strategist at Goldman Sachs, examines the broad ripple effects of the costly trade dispute between the US and its closest trading partners, and how they will ultimately trickle down to American citizens

•  Jean Boivin, Head of the BlackRock Investment Institute, breaks down the different potential outcomes of the trade war between the US and its top economic partners, and the impact of a "stagflationary" shock from the newly imposed tariffs
--------

items.title: DOGE Is Doing Tremendous Damage, Rubin Says
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Former US Treasury Secretary Robert Rubin says Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency is hurting the government. “I think DOGE is doing tremendous damage to government and to the recipients of government services and government activities,” he said. Raymond J. McGuire, Lazard president, also speaks at Bloomberg Invest in New York.

items.title: New Trade War With Canada: What's the Impact?
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>President Donald Trump delivered on his threat to hit Canada and Mexico with sweeping import levies and doubled an existing charge on China, spurring swift reprisals that plunged the world economy into a deepening trade war.

The new US tariffs — 25% duties on most Canadian and Mexican imports and raising the charge on China to 20% — apply to roughly $1.5 trillion in annual imports, an expansive move signaling to markets that the Republican president is committed to inflicting economic pain to generate fresh revenue and create domestic manufacturing jobs.

Columbia Business School professor and former Goldman Sachs senior investment strategist Abby Joseph Cohen explains why she sees this trade war as "dangerous" for the United States and how it harkens back to the economy of the 19th century. Professor Cohen speaks with Tom Keene and Jess Menton on Bloomberg Radio.

Get more on the Bloomberg Surveillance Podcast:

items.title: Ukraine's Zelenskiy Regrets How Trump Meeting Went, Is Committed to Peace
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>President Volodymyr Zelenskiy says Ukraine is ready to negotiate with Russia to end the war. He says the meeting with President Donald Trump last week did not go as planned. “It is regrettable that it happened this way. It is time to make things right. We would like future cooperation and communication to be constructive," he said in a post on X. Matt Miller and Katie Greifeld report.
--------
